message:
fix(http): include non-default port in Host header and sanitize user-agent

Two HTTP correctness fixes:

1. Host header now includes port when non-default. New host_header_value()
   properly constructs the Host header: wraps IPv6 in brackets, appends
   port only when it differs from scheme default (80/HTTP, 443/HTTPS).

2. User-Agent header injection prevention. Overridden user-agent values
   now pass through sanitize_header_value() to prevent CRLF injection.

Also gates DEFAULT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECS with #[cfg(not(test))] to
suppress dead-code warning in test builds.

Tests: build_request_bytes_non_default_port_includes_port_in_host_header,
build_request_bytes_sanitizes_overridden_user_agent

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
